title: Plotting external data
text: Three regions, three p-values: Pass data geom_brain() data argument map fill variable: Brain plot three regions coloured p-value. Regions data appear NA (grey default). Regions data don’t match atlas silently dropped, watch spelling.
code: some_data <- tibble(   region = c("superior temporal", "precentral", "lateral orbitofrontal"),   p = c(0.03, 0.6, 0.05) ) some_data ## # A tibble: 3 × 2 ##   region                    p ##   <chr>                 <dbl> ## 1 superior temporal      0.03 ## 2 precentral             0.6  ## 3 lateral orbitofrontal  0.05 ggplot() +   geom_brain(atlas = dk(), data = some_data, mapping = aes(fill = p))

title: Plotting external data
text: data hemisphere-specific, add hemi column. join use region hemi, values land correct side: Brain plot restricted left hemisphere using hemi column. works atlas column – adding view, instance, restrict matches specific views.
code: some_data$hemi <- "left"  ggplot() +   geom_brain(atlas = dk(), data = some_data, mapping = aes(fill = p))

title: Plotting external data
text: data grouping variable, group facet_wrap() / facet_grid() work ’d expect. geom_brain() replicates full atlas – context regions included – panel: Brain plots faceted age group custom colour gradient. Grouping data tells geom many copies atlas make – one per group.
code: some_data <- tibble(   region = rep(     c(       "transverse temporal",       "insula",       "precentral",       "superior parietal"     ),     2   ),   p = sample(seq(0, 0.5, 0.001), 8),   group = c(rep("Young", 4), rep("Old", 4)) )  ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     data = group_by(some_data, group),     colour = "white",     mapping = aes(fill = p)   ) +   facet_wrap(~group, ncol = 1) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   scale_fill_gradientn(     colours = c("royalblue", "firebrick", "goldenrod"),     na.value = "grey"   )

title: Plotting external data
text: Regions don’t show . Check spelling case. ggseg.formats::atlas_regions(dk()) gives exact strings atlas expects. Data lands hemispheres. Add hemi column "left" "right" constrain match. facet panel missing context. Group data faceting variable plotting (data = my_data |> group_by(group)) full atlas replicated every panel.

title: Plotting external data
text: need layer brain data sf geoms, join atlas manually plotting, work atlas sf object instead. See vignette("geom-sf") workflow.

title: Reading FreeSurfer stats files
text: recon-finishes, subject stats/ folder parcellation data. read_freesurfer_stats() reads one file time: hemisphere encoded filename (lh. rh.). match atlas, prepend label column:
code: subjects_dir <- Sys.getenv("SUBJECTS_DIR") stats_file <- file.path(subjects_dir, "bert/stats/lh.aparc.stats")  data <- read_freesurfer_stats(stats_file) data ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     data = data |> mutate(label = paste0("lh_", label)),     mapping = aes(fill = ThickAvg)   )

title: Reading FreeSurfer stats files
text: read_atlas_files() reads matching stats files subjects directory. Pass regular expression select right files: trailing $ matters – ensures get aparc.stats aparc.a2009s.stats. function adds hemisphere prefixes automatically, data ready plotting: show metrics , pivot facet:
code: dat <- read_atlas_files(subjects_dir, "aparc.stats$") dat ggplot() +   geom_brain(atlas = dk(), data = dat, mapping = aes(fill = ThickStd)) library(tidyr)  long <- dat |>   pivot_longer(-c(subject, label), names_to = "stat", values_to = "val")  ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     data = group_by(long, stat),     mapping = aes(fill = val)   ) +   facet_wrap(~stat)

title: Reading FreeSurfer stats files
text: FreeSurfer’s aparcstats2table asegstats2table commands create summary tables across subjects. Read read_freesurfer_table(): output three columns: subject, label, value.
code: table_path <- "path/to/aparc.volume.table"  read_freesurfer_table(table_path)

title: Reading FreeSurfer stats files
text: Stats tables append measure name label (e.g. lh_bankssts_volume). measure argument strips suffix renames value column:
code: dat <- read_freesurfer_table(table_path, measure = "volume") dat

title: Reading FreeSurfer stats files
text: FreeSurfer tables include summary measures (total volume, ICV) don’t correspond atlas regions. Filter plotting: grepl("lh|rh", label) pattern keeps hemisphere-specific rows.
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     data = dat |> filter(grepl("lh|rh", label)),     mapping = aes(fill = volume)   )

title: Working with geom_sf directly
text: Brain plot using geom_sf brain_join.
code: some_data |>   brain_join(dk()) |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = p)) #> Merging atlas and data by region.

title: Working with geom_sf directly
text: geom_brain(), ’d use position_brain(). sf workflow, use reposition_brain() instead – transforms geometry directly: Repositioned brain views using reposition_brain() geom_sf. formula syntax, results.
code: some_data |>   brain_join(dk()) |>   reposition_brain(hemi ~ view) |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = p)) #> Merging atlas and data by region.

title: Working with geom_sf directly
text: main reason use sf workflow. repositioned sf data, can layer sf geom: Brain regions text labels overlaid using geom_sf_label. crowded plots, ggrepel::geom_label_repel() avoids overlapping labels.
code: some_data |>   brain_join(dk()) |>   reposition_brain(hemi ~ view) |>   ggplot(aes(fill = p)) +   geom_sf(show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_sf_label(     aes(label = ifelse(!is.na(p), region, NA)),     alpha = 0.8,     show.legend = FALSE   ) #> Merging atlas and data by region. #> Warning: Removed 168 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_label()`).

title: Working with geom_sf directly
text: sf workflow, faceting requires group_by() brain_join(). grouping tells join replicate atlas group: Faceted brain plots using geom_sf workflow grouped data. step needed sf workflow. geom_brain() handles atlas replication automatically (see vignette("external-data")).
code: some_data <- tibble(   region = rep(     c(       "transverse temporal",       "insula",       "precentral",       "superior parietal"     ),     2   ),   p = sample(seq(0, 0.5, 0.001), 8),   group = c(rep("A", 4), rep("B", 4)) )  some_data |>   group_by(group) |>   brain_join(dk()) |>   reposition_brain(hemi ~ view) |>   ggplot(aes(fill = p)) +   geom_sf(show.legend = FALSE) +   facet_wrap(~group) #> Merging atlas and data by region.

title: Getting started with ggseg
text: geom_brain() works like ggplot2 geom. Pass atlas draws regions: Default brain plot using geom_brain() dk atlas.
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(atlas = dk(), show.legend = FALSE)

title: Getting started with ggseg
text: Brain atlases multiple views (lateral, medial) hemispheres. position_brain() controls ’re laid , using formula syntax borrowed facet_grid(): Brain views arranged using formula syntax position_brain(). reorder views character vector: Brain views arranged using character vector specify order. See vignette("positioning-views") full set layout options, including grid layouts subcortical tract atlases per-view zoom.
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     position = position_brain(hemi ~ view),     show.legend = FALSE   ) ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     position = position_brain(c(       "right lateral",       "right medial",       "left lateral",       "left medial"     )),     show.legend = FALSE   )

what: Showing only certain hemispheres or views
title: Getting started with ggseg
text: Brain plot showing lateral views. Brain plot showing left hemisphere. subcortical atlases, views slice identifiers. Check ’s available ggseg.formats::atlas_views():
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(atlas = dk(), view = "lateral", show.legend = FALSE) ggplot() +   geom_brain(atlas = dk(), hemi = "left") atlas_views(aseg()) #> [1] "axial_3"   "axial_4"   "axial_5"   "sagittal"  "axial_6"   "coronal_1" #> [7] "coronal_2"

title: Getting started with ggseg
text: Create data frame least one column matches atlas (typically region label). Pass geom_brain() data argument joins atlas automatically: Brain plot coloured external p-values using viridis scale. See vignette("external-data") data preparation matching.
code: library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  some_data <- tibble(   region = c(     "transverse temporal",     "insula",     "precentral",     "superior parietal"   ),   p = sample(seq(0, 0.5, 0.001), 4) )  ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     data = some_data,     position = position_brain(hemi ~ view),     aes(fill = p)   ) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis", direction = -1) +   theme_void()

title: Getting started with ggseg
text: ggplot2 faceting works ’d expect. Group data faceting variable geom_brain() replicates full atlas panel: Brain plots faceted group, panel showing full atlas.
code: some_data <- tibble(   region = rep(     c(       "transverse temporal",       "insula",       "precentral",       "superior parietal"     ),     2   ),   p = sample(seq(0, 0.5, 0.001), 8),   group = c(rep("A", 4), rep("B", 4)) )  ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     data = group_by(some_data, group),     position = position_brain(hemi ~ view),     aes(fill = p)   ) +   facet_wrap(~group)

title: Getting started with ggseg
text: colour regions atlas colours, use two columns: one join (region) one fill aesthetic: Highlighting selected regions using atlas colour palette.
code: data <- data.frame(   region = atlas_regions(dk())[1:3],   reg_col = atlas_labels(dk())[1:3] )  palette <- atlas_palette(dk())[1:3]  ggplot() +   geom_brain(atlas = dk(), data = data, aes(fill = reg_col)) +   scale_fill_brain_manual(palette)

title: Getting started with ggseg
text: Atlas colour palettes applied automatically geom_brain(). custom palettes, use scale_fill_brain_manual(). Built-themes strip axes grids cleaner figures:
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(atlas = dk()) +   theme_brain()

title: Getting started with ggseg
text: full control data reaches ggplot2 — adding region labels, layering sf geoms, using geom_sf() directly — see vignette("geom-sf").

title: Getting started with ggseg
text: ggseg ships dk() (cortical), aseg() (subcortical), suit() (cerebellar flamap), tracula() (white matter tracts). Many available ggsegverse r-universe:
code: install.packages("ggsegYeo2011", repos = "https://ggsegverse.r-universe.dev")

title: Arranging brain views
text: Cortical atlases like dk two layout dimensions: hemi (left right) view (lateral, medial, etc.). formula syntax mirrors facet_grid() – left side rows, right side columns: Cortical atlas hemispheres rows views columns. Flip formula transpose layout: Transposed layout views rows hemispheres columns.
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     position = position_brain(hemi ~ view),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   theme_void() ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     position = position_brain(view ~ hemi),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   theme_void()

title: Arranging brain views
text: Use . + collapse everything single row column. handy compact figures: brain views stacked single row. brain views stacked single column.
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     position = position_brain(. ~ hemi + view),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   theme_void() ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = dk(),     position = position_brain(hemi + view ~ .),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   theme_void()

title: Arranging brain views
text: Subcortical atlases like aseg tract atlases like tracula don’t hemisphere/view pairing cortical atlases . views individual slices (e.g. "axial_3", "sagittal"). opens different set positioning tools.

title: Arranging brain views
text: simplest options. "horizontal" default: Subcortical atlas views arranged horizontally. Subcortical atlas views arranged vertically.
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = aseg(),     position = position_brain("horizontal"),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   theme_void() ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = aseg(),     position = position_brain("vertical"),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   theme_void()

title: Arranging brain views
text: many views, grid keeps things readable. Specify nrow, ncol, : Subcortical atlas views two-row grid. Subcortical atlas views three-column grid.
code: ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = aseg(),     position = position_brain(nrow = 2),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   theme_void() ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = aseg(),     position = position_brain(ncol = 3),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   theme_void()

what: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggseg
title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggseg
text: Purpose: help AI get productive quickly outlining project’s shape, important files, build/test workflows, repo-specific conventions. Keep changes minimal always reference files named making edits. ggseg provides ggplot2 geom data plotting brain atlases. core pieces : R code (R/), packaged atlas data (data/), data preprocessing scripts (data-raw/). package publishes pkgdown site docs/ includes vignettes vignettes/. Spatial functionality depends sf native libs: GDAL, GEOS, PROJ. Changes touching spatial code may require native libs. README.md — high-level overview install examples. DESCRIPTION — package metadata, native deps (SystemRequirements), suggested workflow (roxygen, testthat settings). R/ — implementation. Start ggseg.R, geom-brain.R, brain_atlas.R, read_freesurfer.R main data plotting flows. data/ — prebuilt atlas .rda datasets (e.g. dk.rda, aseg.rda). data-raw/ — scripts generate atlas data: make_data.R, make_hex.R. Treat source--truth atlas creation. vignettes/, docs/, pkgdown/ — docs site + examples. inst/ contains CITATION install-time artifacts. Update docs changing roxygen: devtools::document() Run full package checks: devtools::check() R CMD check . Run unit tests: devtools::test() (testthat 3rd edition; tests run parallel via Config/testthat/parallel: true). Build site locally: pkgdown::build_site() (site lives docs/). Use roxygen2 documentation; RoxygenNote DESCRIPTION indicates expected roxygen version. Atlas data generated data-raw/ stored (compressed) data/ .rda files — hand-edit files data/. Spatial code uses sf idioms tidyverse style. Prefer |> dplyr/tidyr utilities existing code . Tests follow testthat v3 patterns. Keep tests fast; heavy spatial integration can isolated behind small unit tests. sf requires GDAL/GEOS/PROJ system — CI local dev must installed. SystemRequirements DESCRIPTION lists minima (GDAL >= 2.0.1, GEOS >= 3.4.0, PROJ >= 4.8.0). package sets C++17 DESCRIPTION; touch C++ src/, ensure src/Makevars uses CXX_STD = CXX17 check compiler flags. Project uses lintr; add .lintr ignore large auto-generated folders like data-raw/ already ignored: exclude: "data-raw/". PDF manual requires LaTeX packages (e.g. inconsolata); expect PDF creation fail systems missing TeX packages — CI may differ local systems. Modify R code (e.g. R/geom-brain.R). Update roxygen comments file. Run devtools::document() devtools::test(). Run devtools::check() fix warnings/notes PR. anything unclear want file shortened/extended, tell focus iterate.

title: ggseg 2.2.1.9000 (development)
text: geom_brain() respects data aesthetics set top-level ggplot() call. default polygon renderer built atlas eagerly, plot existed, never saw inherited data/aes() fell back colouring region label — e.g. ggplot(df, aes(fill = value)) + geom_brain(atlas = dk()) errored “Discrete value supplied continuous scale”. atlas now flattened joined plot-build time, inherited mappings, inherited data, faceting work (#158).

title: ggseg 2.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-03 test suite now builds sf fixtures public atlas accessors instead reaching atlas internals, tests longer break internal atlas layout changes.

title: ggseg 2.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-22 release makes sf package optional. ggseg now draws brains lightweight polygon representation default, installs plots even systems sf (GDAL / GEOS / PROJ system libraries) unavailable — including WebAssembly air-gapped setups.

title: ggseg 2.2.0
text: plotting code keeps working, without sf. geom_brain(), position_brain(), annotate_brain() produce figures , now drawn without sf. sf moved Imports Suggests. Need full sf toolkit? add region labels geom_sf_label(), layer sf geoms, wrangle geometry directly, convert atlas as_sf_atlas() use ggplot2::geom_sf(). See vignette("geom-sf"). sf-backed geom_brain_sf() position_brain_sf() remain transition period deprecated removed future release.

title: ggseg 2.2.0
text: Zoom regions interest. position_brain(zoom = ...) crops view onto regions ’re highlighting fill panel, surrounding brain reduced tidy grey frame. Use zoom = TRUE follow regions data, name explicitly; zoom_pad sets margin. Especially handy focus atlases structures carry values. Readable view labels. annotate_brain() now places labels clear brain instead top . new padding argument (5% plot height default) controls gap. One annotation function. annotate_brain() works whichever position gave geom — ’s separate labelling function remember. Hide context regions. geom_brain(context = FALSE) drops grey, unlabelled regions tightens layout around regions ’re plotting. Faceting. Group data dplyr::group_by() facet_wrap() / facet_grid() draw full atlas every panel. FreeSurfer labels. Data keyed label (e.g. "lh_bankssts") now joins atlas directly, addition region.

title: ggseg 2.2.0
text: fill mapped, geom_brain() fills regions atlas’s colours, stray “shared levels” warning appeared filtering hemisphere view gone. New coord_brain() keeps brain proportions undistorted; geom_brain() applies automatically, rarely need add . deprecated scale_brain() family keeps working current ggseg.formats. suit cerebellar atlas re-exported alongside dk(), aseg(), tracula().

title: ggseg 2.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-16 Fix minor bug ggproto

title: ggseg 2.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-03 Support cerebellar atlas type 2D view stacking. Cerebellar atlases now use stacking layout subcortical atlases position_brain().

title: ggseg 2.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-19 major release simplifies package architecture moving atlas data structures utilities ggseg.formats package.

title: ggseg 2.0.0
text: ggseg() now defunct errors immediately. Use ggplot() + geom_brain() instead. Atlas data (dk, aseg) longer bundled ggseg. Atlases now provided ggseg.formats re-exported functions: dk(), aseg(), tracula(). Code using bare objects (e.g., atlas = dk) must updated atlas = dk(). following functions removed now ggseg.formats: as_brain_atlas(), is_brain_atlas(), brain_atlas(), brain_regions(), brain_labels(), brain_pal(), brain_pals_info(), ggseg_atlas(), as_ggseg_atlas(), is_ggseg_atlas(), read_freesurfer_stats(), read_freesurfer_table(), read_atlas_files(). scale_brain2(), scale_fill_brain2(), scale_colour_brain2(), scale_color_brain2() deprecated favour scale_brain_manual(), scale_fill_brain_manual(), scale_colour_brain_manual(), scale_color_brain_manual(). scale_brain(), scale_fill_brain(), scale_colour_brain(), scale_color_brain() deprecated. Atlas palettes now applied automatically geom_brain(). side argument geom_brain() position_brain() renamed view.

title: ggseg 2.0.0
text: New annotate_brain() function adds view labels (e.g., “left lateral”) brain plots, respecting layout position_brain(). New scale_brain_manual() family applying custom named colour palettes brain plots. position_brain() gains nrow, ncol, views arguments grid-based layout control subcortical tract atlases. adapt_scales() now accepts atlas objects directly (just pre-converted coordinate data frames), handles "tract" atlas types alongside subcortical. geom_brain() now automatically applies atlas colour palette fill aesthetic mapped.

title: ggseg 2.0.0
text: Messaging uses cli user-facing output (brain_join() warnings info messages). Rewrote reorganised vignettes updated examples renamed files cleaner URLs. Added tracula (white matter tract) atlas re-export ggseg.formats. Improved documentation throughout updated roxygen2 docs.
